member.name: Cameron Thomas
member.synopsis: Cameron Thomas is the Independent MP for Tewkesbury , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Events Industry: Conditions of Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to improve working conditions for those working in the live events and production industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Employment Rights Act will benefit over 18 million workers across the country, including those in the creative sector . Changes include ending one-sided flexibility for workers on zero-hours or similar contracts by introducing a right to guaranteed ho...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Road Traffic Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to improve traffic management and congestion patterns on motorways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways is responsible for operating motorways in England. Through the third Road Investment Strategy (RIS3), published in March 2026, the Government has set National Highways several targeted key performance indicators covering; average delay, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Safety Measures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what preventative measures her Department is considering to reduce serious collisions and improve motorway safety.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Road safety is a matter the Government takes very seriously and National Highways is committed to reducing the number of people killed or seriously injured on England’s motorways and major A-roads. National Highways works closely with the Police and other...

writtenQuestions.heading: M5: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department conducts regular strategic reviews of collision hotspots and fatal incidents on the M5.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Road safety is a matter the Government takes very seriously and National Highways is committed to reducing the number of people killed or seriously injured on England’s motorways and major A-roads. National Highways works closely with the Police and other...

writtenQuestions.heading: M5: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to bring down the number of serious and fatal collisions on the M5 motorway.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Road safety is a matter the Government takes very seriously and National Highways is committed to reducing the number of people killed or seriously injured on England’s motorways and major A-roads. National Highways works closely with the Police and other...

writtenQuestions.heading: M5: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the potential for the proposed M5 Junction 9 and A46 (Ashchurch) Transport Scheme to mitigate collisions on the M5.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Gloucestershire County Council’s proposals for this scheme have focused on developing a relief road to mitigate increased traffic from the planned Tewkesbury Garden Community and to offer relief to Junction 9 of the M5. The traffic modelling for the prop...

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure NHS contractors such as HealthHarmonie are regularly monitored.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Care Quality Commission (CQC) registers, inspects, and monitors health and care providers in England.Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for assuring the quality, effectiveness, and value of the services they commission for their populations...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Ashchurch for Tewkesbury Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of a third platform at Ashchurch for Tewkesbury Station on supporting growth in the Gloucestershire area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department currently has no plans to provide a third platform at Ashchurch for Tewkesbury station. We encourage Network Rail, train operating companies, and local stakeholders to develop the case for future station improvements for consideration at fu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ashchurch for Tewkesbury Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a third platform at Ashchurch for Tewkesbury Station to support business travel between Bristol and Birmingham.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department currently has no plans to provide a third platform at Ashchurch for Tewkesbury station. We encourage Network Rail, train operating companies, and local stakeholders to develop the case for future station improvements for consideration at fu...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department currently has no plans to provide a third platform at Ashchurch for Tewkesbury station. We encourage Network Rail, train operating companies, and local stakeholders to develop the case for future station improvements for consideration at fu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Respite Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment has the Department made of adequacy of respite breaks for unpaid carers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the vital role unpaid carers play and is committed to ensuring they have access to the support they need, including opportunities for respite.Under the Care Act 2014, local authorities have a duty to assess carers who appear to h...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Medical Records: Data Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps is he taking to strengthen protections against unauthorised access to patient data.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All organisations that have access to National Health Service patient data and systems must use the Data Security and Protection Toolkit (DSPT) to provide assurance on an annual basis that they are practising good data security and that personal informati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure patient data privacy regarding the use of AI software in the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that patient data is protected to the highest standards as artificial intelligence (AI) is adopted across the National Health Service. All use of data remains subject to UK General Data Protection Regulation, suppor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Medical Records: Data Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to limit the transfer of patient data to overseas contractors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are strong protections in law to ensure that health and care information is used in a safe, secure, and legal way. This includes the UK General Data Protection Regulation and Data Protection Act 2018, which establishes a legal framework for processi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether the current in-development framework regarding the regulation of AI in healthcare will introduce a medical device classification for AI software requiring external assessment, and whether t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) is currently undertaking a programme of regulatory reform for medical devices. This includes ensuring there is proportionate regulation of artificial intelligence (AI) as a medical device, whi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability: Equality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what safeguards exist to help that ensure procedural compliance with the Draft Code of Practice for Services, Public Functions and Associations (2026) does not replace substantive disability rights.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Equality and Human Rights Commissions’ (EHRC’s) updated statutory guidance - the draft Code of Practice for Services, Public Functions and Associations (the “Code”) provides guidance to service providers, associations and those exercising public funct...

writtenQuestions.heading: Liver Diseases: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve (a) treatment and (b) care for patients suffering from non-alcoholic cirrhosis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Liver health and liver cancer remain a priority for the National Health Service. NHS England has established a liver transformation programme focussing on awareness, prevention, diagnosis, detection, and treatment of all forms of liver disease, such as ci...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Liver Diseases: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve diagnosis for non-alcoholic cirrhosis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Liver health and liver cancer remain a priority for the National Health Service. NHS England has established a liver transformation programme focussing on awareness, prevention, diagnosis, detection, and treatment of all forms of liver disease, such as ci...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Liver Diseases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans his Department has to increase funding for liver disease, including non-alcoholic cirrhosis care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Services to manage and treat patients with liver disease are commissioned at a local level and are considered alongside other healthcare priorities. Specific investment in services is decided locally, reflecting local population need.The liver transformat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
